deck: Dimagi's Lilianna Bagnoli on resilience as a learnable skill, and how WellMe, a CommCare-based app, aims to guard frontline workers against burnout across languages, geographies, and cadres.
seo_description: Lilianna Bagnoli on building resilience to combat frontline worker burnout, and how Dimagi's WellMe app delivers mental health support at scale.
og_description: Lilianna Bagnoli on resilience, burnout, and how Dimagi's WellMe app supports frontline worker mental health at scale.
about: In a world where stress is inevitable and burnout is rife, resilience is the acquired ability to recover, adapt, and grow from stress. In this episode, Jonathan Jackson sits down with Dimagi's Lilianna Bagnoli, Associate Director of Delivery for the India division and the team's informal mental health lead, to unpack how building resilience can guard frontline workers against burnout, defined by the World Health Organization as fatigue, detachment from work, and reduced professional efficacy.

Lilianna lays out the global picture, where about one in eight people live with a mental disorder yet specialist care is in short supply, and walks through the four interventions advocated by the 2018 Lancet Commission: task sharing, digital technologies, the balanced care model, and increasing demand. She then introduces WellMe, Dimagi's newly launched application built on CommCare that equips frontline workers with resilience-building behaviors through short courses, journal prompts, quizzes, and practice activities.

The conversation digs into hard lessons from user testing with drivers and delivery partners in Delhi, where the value proposition of mental health support collided with the daily pressure to earn enough to get by, and closes with a candid reflection on resilience at the organizational level, from incentives and fair pay to Dimagi's own efforts to take care of its staff.

pull_quote.text: Addressing just the individual level, which is what we are doing with resilience, is important, but shouldn't be confused for being sufficient as a response to the stressors that frontline workers face.

bullets: Resilience as a skill: why it can be learned and practiced, and how it acts as a buffer against chronic workplace stress
Defining burnout: the WHO's three markers of fatigue, detachment, and reduced professional efficacy as a workplace phenomenon
Four proven interventions: task sharing, digital technologies, the balanced care model, and increasing demand, drawn from the 2018 Lancet Commission
Introducing WellMe: Dimagi's CommCare-based app delivering resilience courses, journal prompts, quizzes, and practice activities
Lessons from Delhi: what drivers and delivery partners revealed about value, language, and resistance to talking about stress
Whose responsibility: why resilience-building belongs in job training, and the case for paying workers to build it
Resilience at every level: from frontline workers to Dimagi's own staff, including rest days, coaching, and honest conversations

frontline workers WellMe is being piloted with this year across geographies and sectors

guests.name: Lilianna Bagnoli
guests.role: Associate Director of Delivery for Dimagi's India division and the team's informal mental health lead, driving its resilience-building work and the WellMe application.
